response.justification: James Smith is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with strong web and application engineering credentials, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload optimization — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency async I/O work at Dunnhumby and some embedded/hardware-adjacent work with robotic arms, but these fall far short of the hardware performance engineering and silicon-level architecture skills required at a principal IC level.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across multiple domains, demonstrating adaptability and technical depth
Low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, high-performance databases at Dunnhumby) shows some performance-aware mindset
Embedded and hardware-adjacent experience (robotic arms, mobile device orchestration, C/C++ at National Instruments) provides a thin foundation
concerns: Zero demonstrated experience in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator design — the core technical pillars of this role
No background in deep-learning workload analysis, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or hardware-software co-design for ML systems
Entire career arc is web/application engineering; there is no trajectory toward hardware performance or silicon-level architecture work

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or GPU architecture experience
